business_ideas.idea: CB Insights: credit card delinquency sentiment surveys for hedge funds at $12-50-100k tiers
Razor's Edge: high-net-worth donor database for charities, evolved to CRM platform
High-end Glassdoor: CEO leadership quality signal via exec interviews for hedge funds
Founder option contracts: pay founders cash now for right to invest in their next company
School of entrepreneurship: 6-12 boarding school teaching business not traditional academics

predictions.text: CB Insights could grow beyond $800M valuation with AI and data products expansion

disagreements: Debate on whether recruiting service or data service is better business model; Anand argues services better than pure data for behavioral change adoption
dynamics: Anand leads with founder story and strategic thinking; Sam asks probing follow-ups on business mechanics. Deep analytical conversation about data businesses, founder psychology, education reform. Anand philosophically grounded; Sam practically curious.
frameworks: Edge-Collection-Opportunity (ECO) framework: define edge (competitive advantage), feasibility to collect data, market opportunity size
Data cooperatives: pooled data + workflow tools create stickiness beyond pure data sale
From data to services: data alone hard to sell; add application layer (CRM, workflow) for adoption
Long-term stock exchanges: require regulatory approval, overcome quarterly earnings pressure
Competency-based education over subject-based: teach thinking skills via real projects
The Lean Startup principle: launch minimal, iterate based on real feedback, avoid big launches
Big idea execution: publicly kill assumptions to keep team aligned on pivots
learnings: Data itself valueless; edge and application matter more than raw data
Trust is gating factor for data businesses; warm introductions and relationships critical
Hedge funds biggest data buyers because they make large decisions with asymmetric information
Bootstrapping for 6 years before Series A enabled founders to retain equity and control
Long-term thinking requires structural changes (remove quarterly earnings, add founder incentives)
School building requires solving founder recruitment (how to identify entrepreneurial kids young)
Effective education combines experiential learning with mentorship from practitioners
Financial bootstrapping demonstrates unit economics before institutional capital
Venture capital stamp of approval has outsized effect on sales momentum

banger_quotes.quote: Data itself is not valuable. It's what you can do with data that makes it valuable.
This is what they're going to care about in the future, not what they care about today
If you have data from one hack, you're dead when you lose access
Show me your incentives and I'll show you your outcome
Children of deadbeat dads grow up tough and independent
Find the people you love and do life with them

numbers_dropped: Credit card PDFs: $700k revenue in one year
Soft dollar prime broker deals: $12k-$50k-$100k pricing tiers
Hedge fund sentiment survey: 20-25 people sampled
Bootstrapped 6 years before Series A fundraising
notable: Discussion of effective altruism and FTX collapse provides context for how charismatic founders can misuse ideology; shows importance of verification vs assumption.
